summary: Matched the common fighter Captain Falcon capture state file `ftCo_CaptureCaptain` at 100%, replacing its assembly in `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` with C and adding it to the build. The PR also cleaned up nearby fighter struct typing by converting `Fighter::victim_gobj`/`x1A5C` to `Fighter_GObj*`, splitting the opaque `x2226_flag.bits.bN` byte into direct bitfields `x2226_b0` through `x2226_b7`, and updating users across fighter/common/draw/init code. No review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Removed the matching assembly bodies for `ftCo_8009CA0C`, `ftCo_CaptureCaptain_Anim`, `ftCo_CaptureCaptain_IASA`, `ftCo_CaptureCaptain_Phys`, and `ftCo_CaptureCaptain_Coll` from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`.
Added `ftCo_CaptureCaptain.c.o` to `TEXT_O_FILES` in `obj_files.mk` before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o` assembly object.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureCaptain.h` with prototypes and address comments for the matched functions.
Included `ftCommon/ftCo_CaptureCaptain.h` from Captain's `ftCa_SpecialHi.c`, and removed the old `ftCo_8009CA0C` declaration from `ft_08A4.h`.
Changed `Fighter::victim_gobj` and `Fighter::x1A5C` from generic `HSD_GObj*` to `Fighter_GObj*` in `types.h`.

Replaced `Fighter::x2226_flag` as an `UnkFlagStruct` with explicit `u8` bitfields `x2226_b0` through `x2226_b7`, then updated usages in fighter init, damage, draw, library, common, Koopa/GigaKoopa/Yoshi/Sandbag init, and DownBound code.
Updated `ftcommon.h` prototypes to use `ftCo_Fighter*` and `ftCo_GObj*` aliases for common-fighter APIs, supported by adding `ftCommon/forward.h`.
smart_moves: The matched C mirrors the assembly's dataflow directly: stores both `victim_gobj` and `x1A5C`, clears `x221B_b5`, flips `facing_dir` from the victim, branches on `ground_or_air == GA_Air`, passes `fp->victim_gobj` as the final `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` argument, and installs `ftCo_800DB464` only when `x221B_b7` is false.
Preserved a matching-only unused stack artifact in `ftCo_8009CA0C` with `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` and a `@todo Unused stack` note instead of obscuring the real logic.
Moved the new function declaration into a dedicated `ftCo_CaptureCaptain.h` rather than leaving it in the broad `ft_08A4.h` bucket, improving ownership for future ftCommon action-state files.
Split the byte at `fp+2226` into named offset/bit bitfields, making code like `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)->x2226_b2` able to match the same byte tests while avoiding verbose `x2226_flag.bits.b2` access.
Typed capture-related GObj fields as `Fighter_GObj*`, which fits their usage in the new capture code and reduces generic `HSD_GObj*` ambiguity.
decomp_lessons: When matching a small action-state file, remove only the corresponding assembly symbols and keep the remainder of the assembly object in the build until all neighboring functions are matched.
For common fighter state files, create a colocated `ftCo_*` header with forward declarations and include it from call sites instead of growing catch-all headers.
Direct one-byte bitfields with address/bit comments can be preferable to an `UnkFlagStruct` wrapper when codegen or readability depends on simple `fp->xNNNN_bM` access.
Capture-state matching may require struct cleanup outside the new file; in this PR, correctly typing victim object fields and x2226 bits was part of making the C natural and reusable.
Empty callbacks should be represented as empty C functions when the assembly body is just `blr`; this preserves the action-state function table shape without unnecessary logic.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action-state files use `ftCo_<State>.c` and `ftCo_<State>.h`, here `ftCo_CaptureCaptain`.
Unidentified but offset-known fighter bitfields are named as `x<offset>_b<bit>`, e.g. `x2226_b0` through `x2226_b7`.
Common fighter object and fighter aliases are used as `ftCo_GObj*` and `ftCo_Fighter*` in ftCommon-facing declarations and implementation.
Matched headers used an address-style guard `GALE01_09CA0C` and address comments such as `/* 09CA0C */`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the assembly sequence at `8009CA0C` into structured C while preserving call order: `ftCommon_8007DB58`, `ftCo_8009750C`, `ftCo_800DD168`, `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, `ftCommon_8007E2F4`, `ftAnim_8006EBA4`, `ftCommon_8007E2FC`.
Represented the assembly's `rlwimi` bit writes at offset `0x221B` as named bitfield assignments `x221B_b5` and `x221B_b7`.
Matched `fneg`/store of victim facing direction as `fp->facing_dir = -vic_fp->facing_dir`.
Matched `ftCo_CaptureCaptain_Coll`'s byte test at `0x2226` with `if (!GET_FIGHTER(gobj)->x2226_b2) { ft_80083B68(gobj); }`.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` for an unused stack allocation in `ftCo_8009CA0C`, indicating the source needed a matching-only artifact.

Removed the local sdata2 constants `ftCo_804D8760` and `ftCo_804D8764` from the assembly slice because the matched C encodes the corresponding `0` and `1` float arguments directly in the `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` call.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue matching functions remaining in `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` after `ftCo_CaptureCaptain`.
Search for subsequent renames or semantic identification of `x2226_b0` through `x2226_b7`.
Check whether other capture states reuse the `victim_gobj`/`x1A5C` pattern and `x221B_b7` callback setup.
Look for PRs that standardize `ftCo_GObj` and `ftCo_Fighter` aliases across more common fighter headers.
